mapping.a_element: the DuVal singularity C^2/G for a finite G subset SU(2) (ADE type)
mapping.b_element: the singularity C^2/Z_n (the A_{n-1} case of the same Kleinian family)
the resolution of C^2/Z_n whose local geometry sits inside the K3

mapping.a_element: ADE root system canonically associated to G via the McKay correspondence
mapping.b_element: A_{n-1} root system / gauge algebra attached to the geometry, with explicit remarks on the McKay correspondence

mapping.a_element: exceptional curves of the resolution realizing the simple roots
mapping.b_element: exceptional curves of the classical geometry manifesting the simple roots

mapping.a_element: quantum cohomology / quantum product (A-model Gromov-Witten) that encodes the full root-system structure
mapping.b_element: quantum geometry obtained from local mirror symmetry (B-model) that exhibits all positive roots, not just the simple ones

mapping.a_element: equivariant Gromov-Witten potential / Frobenius manifold computed for the resolution
mapping.b_element: D-brane central charges / periods obtained by solving the GKZ system of the local geometry (the mirror-dual generating data)

brief_justification: Both describe the quantum geometry of the resolution of the Kleinian singularity C^2/G and encode it in an ADE root system through the McKay correspondence, with the classical exceptional curves realizing the simple roots and the quantum-corrected structure revealing the full (positive) root system. A computes this via equivariant quantum cohomology (A-model Gromov-Witten) while B computes it via local mirror symmetry and the GKZ period system (B-model); these are mirror-dual computations of the same underlying object, so the machinery role-maps element for element.
